Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need analyze event log cases 1 5 against normative process

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Normative description Always begins with Receive order Next logical step is Check inventory Must occur before any decisions about payment authorization or fulfillment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If inventory sufficient sequence Receive Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order So authorization after check and before packaging Package after auth and before ship Ship after package Close final

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If inventory insufficient after Check inventory Cancel order Once canceled illogical to continue fulfillment activities packaging shipping next reasonable step Close order Sequence Receive Check Cancel Close

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Receive Check Authorize Package Ship Close This is perfect sufficient inventory path No violations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Here check occurs then package before authorize payment Constraint package should only occur after authorization and before shipping Here package before auth violation Also authorize should be before packaging So case 2 violates ordering of authorization and packaging

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 after check they authorize payment then cancel order then ship after cancel Violations Once cancelled should not do fulfillment activities Here they ship after cancel Also they authorized payment then cancelled The path should branch immediately after check Doing both auth and cancel violates the branching logic Also they ship without packaging So multiple violations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Receive Check Cancel Close This is correct cancellation path Close is final

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 they check authorize then ship without packaging items Constraint Package items should occur after authorization and before shipping So missing package step or shipping before packaging So case 5 violates

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus violating cases 2 3 5 Case 1 and 4 correct
